title: First observation on emergence of strong room-temperature ferroelectricity and multiferroicity in 2D-Ti3C2Tx free-standing MXene film

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports room-temperature ferroelectricity and magnetoelectric coupling in a free-standing Ti3C2Tx MXene film after HF etching, LiCl delamination, air exposure, and mild heat treatment that partially oxidizes the MXene to TiO2. The key claim is that a TiO2/Ti3C2Tx composite formed in the film exhibits a polarization hysteresis loop, remanent polarization of 0.5 mC cm−2, and magnetic-field-tunable polarization, suggesting multiferroicity at room temperature.

figures.description: Synthesis scheme for producing delaminated Ti3C2Tx MXene free-standing film.
Photograph/diagram of the sample stage used for ferroelectric/multiferroic testing.
Schematic of the Ti3C2Tx free-standing film mounted with electrical connections between sample-stage pins and the film.
XRD patterns of Ti3AlC2 MAX phase, delaminated Ti3C2Tx MXene, and heat-treated Ti3C2Tx MXene film; inset shows MAX phase pattern.
SEM micrograph of bulk Ti3AlC2 MAX phase.
Cross-sectional SEM image of delaminated Ti3C2Tx MXene film showing separated sheets.
Raman spectra of air-exposed oxidized film and heat-treated samples at 100 C and 300 C, showing anatase, rutile, brookite, and graphitic carbon bands.
Room-temperature polarization-electric field hysteresis loops for samples heated at 100 C and 150 C; inset shows I-V curves for HT@100 C.
Electric field versus time and polarization versus time for HT@100 C, showing remanent polarization behavior.
Dielectric constant versus electric field hysteresis loop for HT@100 C over 1000 cycles, demonstrating reproducibility and stability.
Magnetoelectric coupling coefficient versus magnetic field for HT@100 C measured at room temperature.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports experimental measurements collected from synthesized free-standing Ti3C2Tx films, including XRD scans, SEM images, Raman spectra, ferroelectric P-E hysteresis loops, I-V leakage curves, polarization and electric-field time traces, dielectric hysteresis over 1000 cycles, and magnetoelectric coupling versus magnetic field. The instruments explicitly mentioned include a Radiant Technologies Inc. precision tester for ferroelectric/multiferroic testing; XRD, SEM, and Raman instruments are used but not named in the text provided.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The data will be available on demand.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data are implied to exist for XRD, SEM, Raman, ferroelectric, dielectric, and magnetoelectric measurements, but the paper does not specify file formats, repositories, or downloadable raw datasets.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed/derived values are reported in the text and figures, including c-lattice parameter, d-spacing, remanent polarization (0.5 mC cm−2), saturation polarization values, and qualitative phase assignments from Raman peaks. No explicit tables of numerical source data are provided in the text excerpt.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are shown in the main figures, but the paper does not state that source data files are separately provided. The only explicit availability statement is that data are available on demand.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes between synthesis/processing steps and measurement outputs, but does not provide a formal dataset package. The figures appear to be generated from experimental measurements rather than simulations. Supplementary information is mentioned (ESI available), including Fig. S1 for polarization-voltage measurements of air-exposed and higher-temperature-heated samples, but no explicit supplementary data format is described.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper describes direct experimental measurements: XRD, SEM, Raman, ferroelectric hysteresis, I-V, dielectric hysteresis, and magnetoelectric coupling tests performed on the synthesized films.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Main-text figures present figure-ready experimental curves and images; the paper also references supplementary Fig. S1 and states that data are available on demand, but no repository or script release is described.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data will be available on demand.”; “† Electronic supplementary information (ESI) available.”; “Fig. S1” is referenced for additional polarization-voltage measurements.
